title: PDEBench Scientific Machine-Learning Simulation Benchmark

data_urls.access_notes: Official DaRUS data repository; individual files can be very large.
Smaller hosted subsets are useful for quick SPECTRA smoke tests.
Use for benchmark semantics and citation.

access.authentication: none_expected; full files can be large
instructions: Do not download all PDEBench files by default.
Start with one equation family and a small parameter subset, or use the Hugging Face subsets for smoke tests.
Record equation family, PDE parameters, initial/boundary conditions, grid resolution, time horizon, variables, and split.

scale.download_size: large multi-file simulation benchmark; some individual HDF5 files are multi-GB

recommended_axes.name: pde_parameter_distance
recommended_axes.definition: Distance in physical coefficients such as viscosity, advection speed, reaction parameters, or boundary parameters.

recommended_axes.name: initial_condition_similarity
recommended_axes.definition: Similarity of initial fields using spectra, norms, summary statistics, or learned field embeddings.

recommended_axes.name: regime_or_equation_family_support
recommended_axes.definition: Support over PDE family and regime, such as smooth, discontinuous, shock-dominated, turbulent, or multiphysics behavior.

recommended_axes.name: spatiotemporal_resolution_support
recommended_axes.definition: Distance in grid resolution, time horizon, rollout length, and sampling cadence.

useful_for: Testing whether neural operators generalize over physical parameters rather than only interpolate nearby simulations.
Constructing spectral curves for simulation surrogates over initial-condition and PDE-regime novelty.
Providing a physics-heavy SPECTRA example outside bio and molecules.
leakage_risks: Different time windows from the same simulation run can leak trajectory identity.
Parameter grids can leak if train/test values are interleaved too densely.
Downsampled or regridded versions of the same field should not cross train/test splits.

paperclip_provenance.notes: Paperclip repeatedly surfaced PDEBench in SciML benchmark searches; a targeted map hit a transient Paperclip result lookup issue for s_54517283, so official DaRUS/GitHub/Hugging Face sources were used for access verification.
